question_title: What will be the value of "Moody's Seasoned Baa Corporate Bond Yield Relative to Yield on 10-Year Treasury Constant Maturity" on 2026-03-26?
summary: The BAA10Y spread (Moody's Baa vs 10Y Treasury) was 1.84% on March 12, 2026, well above its 10-month regime mean of 1.73%. A +0.07 spike on March 11 reflects tariff/trade war credit risk repricing, confirmed by parallel widening in ICE BofA BBB OAS. Monte Carlo simulation over 10 trading days using blended empirical drift (+0.0035/day) and volatility (0.028/day) across three scenarios (continued widening, stabilization, partial mean reversion) produces a median near 1.84-1.85 with moderate uncertainty. The distribution is roughly symmetric around current levels with slight upward skew from momentum.

factors.description: Current value at 1.84 on Mar 12, elevated well above the 10-month stable regime mean of 1.729. This anchors the distribution center.
Recent upward momentum: +0.07 jump on Mar 11, 20-day drift of +0.0084/day, ICE BofA BBB OAS also widening. Momentum favors continued widening over 10 trading days.
Mean-reversion tendency: BAA10Y is 0.11pp above regime mean. After credit spread spikes, partial reversion within 2 weeks is common unless fundamentals worsen.
Trade war / tariff macro backdrop creating upside tail risk for spreads. UBS expects pandemic-era widening if tariffs escalate.
Short 10-trading-day horizon limits extreme moves. Daily vol ~0.028 implies ~0.09 one-sigma 10-day range.

reasoning:
Based on my analysis, the BAA10Y spread is currently at 1.84 (as of March 12), significantly above its 10-month stable regime mean of ~1.73. The recent widening is driven by tariff/trade war concerns, with a notable +0.07 single-day spike on March 11. The resolution date of March 26 is approximately 10 trading days away.

My Monte Carlo simulation modeled three weighted scenarios: continued widening (40%), stabilization (35%), and partial mean reversion (25%), using empirical daily volatility of ~0.028 from recent data. The distribution centers near the current value with a slight upward tilt from momentum.
sources_consulted: [ICE BofA BBB US Corporate Index Option-Adjusted Spread (BAMLC0A4CBBB) | FRED | St. Louis Fed](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/BAMLC0A4CBBB)
[ICE BofA US Corporate Index Option-Adjusted Spread (BAMLC0A0CM) | FRED | St. Louis Fed](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/BAMLC0A0CM)
[ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread (BAMLH0A0HYM2) | FRED | St. Louis Fed](https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/BAMLH0A0HYM2)
resolution_criteria: Resolves to the value found on the FRED API for the series BAA10Y once the data is published.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will paginate through the data found at the API endpoint `https://api.stlouisfed.org/fred/series/observations?series_id=BAA10Y`. This endpoint includes the latest data for the series. The latest revised data will be used when the resolution is assessed. The datapoint matching 2026-03-26 will be used to determine the resolution of this question.

A datapoint matches if:
1. The series is updated daily and the date of the datapoint is within 1 day previous to the resolution date.
2. The series is updated weekly and the date of the datapoint is within 7 days previous to the resolution date.
3. The series is updated monthly and the date of the datapoint is within 31 days previous to the resolution date.

If the datapoint is clearly skipped, or no datapoint is found after 2 weeks of checking then the question will be annulled.
